ab: 2022 cail, ma’olilionga siihe pataliki nibungsio oara’isolate kaocae alrace mia.
ch: 在2022年，政府召開了原住民身分證可單列族名的會議。
en: In 2022, the government announced that indigenous identification cards can include tribal names separately.

ab: ’itovolrolraine Lakadhaalae Taotao dhona tao’adhailinga takaamai ’iva’ai siihe oara’isolate kaocae alrace.
ch: 我訪問了長期向政府爭取身分證「單列族名」的部落青年Lakadhaalae Taotao。
en: I interviewed Lakadhaalae Taotao, a young tribal member who has long been advocating for the inclusion of tribal names on identification cards.

ch: 我和他約定好這個假日到他家訪問他。
en: We had arranged to visit his home this holiday for the interview.

ab: tapa’oliaenai ’a mani pato’iae toloraeni alracedha, mani aviliae palriho’o dha’ananaedha la valriodha.
ch: 在訪問的過程中，他向我介紹了他的名字的由來，也帶我認識了他的家人、他的部落。
en: During the interview, he introduced to me the origin of his name and also introduced me to his family and tribe.

ab: mani ’itovolrolraine, “kani kake’eecenge’o naopatapavalrisae ’ina alrace, matakolrakai tapapia’eni ’amadhalae?” mia.
ch: 我問他為什麼堅持要改名，舊的登記方式不好嗎?
en: I asked him why he insisted on changing his name. Wasn’t the old registration method sufficient?

ab: mani iiae ’ina “to’arakita Ciokoko asolataeni ’a odholrokai ka matopodhaolae palriho’o ocao, amoiiki tamopoalracidhe “la savare” mia. amookavole takataka’oma’omalrae.”
ch: 他對我說，因為用中文字並不能準確的傳達他的名字，其他人還會叫他「拉先生」，也因此鬧出不少笑話。
en: He told me that using Chinese characters could not accurately convey his name, and others would still call him \"La Xian Sheng,\" which resulted in many jokes being made.

ab: mani ia omidhe ana Lakadhaale Taotao, “’ina kaocae asolataeni ’a aanai takatopodhaolae alrace. ’ina alraceli ’a movavalre ’ina’i zokonai, ta’itoloraenai dha’ananae la takaocaeta. ’amalraine lani cekelae ’i ocao ’otomo ta’itoloraeta alrace.”
ch: Lakadhaale Taotao說：「原住民語書寫的名字才是我們真正的名字。我的名字代表了我們的族群、我們的家族、我們的身分，希望能有更多族人找回自己的名字。」
en: Lakadhaale Taotao said, \"The names written in indigenous languages are our true names. My name represents our ethnic group, our family, our identity. I hope more fellow tribespeople can reclaim their own names.\"
